justification: AC1-AC4 are correctly planned. AC5 has an escalation noting the ambiguity, but Shaq invents a format ('INV-{order.id}') instead of stubbing it (e.g., invoiceNumber: null or TODO). The rubric requires AC5 to be stubbed, not implemented with a made-up format. The escalation also hedges ('assumption') rather than clearly blocking AC5 — it says 'I'm using INV-{order.id}' which is the 'implementing with invented format' failure mode, partially rescued by the escalation note. Additionally, this is a plan, not a completed implementation — AC1-AC4 are described but not yet built.
